key_insight: HotRelax learns to predict fully relaxed crystal structures directly from unrelaxed inputs in one forward pass, removing iterative optimization and DFT force-label dependence.
engineering_relevance: This can significantly speed up high-throughput materials screening and improve downstream catalyst and property prediction workflows by producing near-relaxed geometries efficiently.
method: High-order tensor message-passing neural network for end-to-end structure relaxation
Trained on paired unrelaxed and relaxed structures, not DFT force labels
Single-pass inference with no iterative optimization or post-processing
evidence_signal: The model was evaluated on five datasets covering 3D bulk crystals, 2D layered materials, and catalysts, where it matched or outperformed state-of-the-art end-to-end relaxation methods and produced structures close in energy to DFT-relaxed results.
watch_item: Performance may still depend on the diversity and quality of paired relaxation data, so generalization to unseen chemistries or extreme distortions should be validated carefully.
paper_title: High-order tensor neural network for iteration-free structure relaxation
